description: Remote Attach Session History Restoration - Fix incomplete message history when attaching to remote sessions

userStories.title: Fix SSE race condition — always perform full sync on session entry
userStories.description: As an observer attaching to a remote session, I want the full session history to load when I enter the session view, even if SSE events have already delivered some recent messages.
acceptanceCriteria: sync.session.sync(sessionID) always runs when navigating to a session, regardless of whether store.message[sessionID] already has entries from SSE events
The sync merges fetched history with any SSE-delivered messages (no duplicates, no gaps) using message ID-based deduplication
fullSyncedSessions flag is only set after a successful full sync, not after partial SSE population
Existing local session navigation continues to work correctly (no regression)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The guard `if (!sync.data.message[sessionId]?.length)` causes the bug — SSE events populate messages before sync runs, so sync gets skipped. The fetched set is authoritative for ordering; SSE-delivered messages newer than the fetched set should be appended.

userStories.title: Remove hard 100-message cap on initial sync
userStories.description: As a user viewing any session (local or remote), I want to see all messages in the session history, not just the latest 100.
acceptanceCriteria: sync.session.sync() fetches all messages for the session (remove limit: 100 or use a sufficiently large default)
Server endpoint GET /session/:sessionID/message supports unlimited fetch when no limit is specified
Performance remains acceptable for sessions with up to 500 messages
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Currently sdk.client.session.messages({ sessionID, limit: 100 }) hard-caps at 100. Check both the SDK call site and the server endpoint for the limit.

userStories.title: Remove rolling window eviction for active sessions
userStories.description: As an observer watching an active session, I want new messages to accumulate without evicting old ones, so I can review the full conversation.
acceptanceCriteria: The message.updated handler no longer evicts the oldest message when count exceeds 100
Raise the in-memory message threshold to 500 as a safety bound
Memory usage remains reasonable for sessions up to 500 messages
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The message.updated event handler currently evicts the oldest message when the in-memory array exceeds 100. Raise to 500 as a safe upper bound until pagination is implemented.

userStories.title: Add cursor-based pagination to session messages API
userStories.description: As a developer, I need cursor-based pagination on the messages API so clients can lazily load older messages.
acceptanceCriteria: Server endpoint GET /session/:sessionID/message supports 'before' query parameter (message ID) for cursor-based pagination
When 'before' is provided, return messages with ID < before, ordered descending, limited to batch size
MessageV2.stream() or equivalent query adds WHERE id < :before clause when cursor provided
Endpoint continues to return all messages when neither limit nor before is specified
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Server-side only. MessageV2.stream() already loads from SQLite ordered by ID. Adding WHERE id < :before is straightforward. TUI scroll-to-top trigger is in the next story.

userStories.title: TUI scroll-to-top lazy loading for older messages
userStories.description: As a user viewing a session with hundreds of messages, I want to load older messages on demand when I scroll to the top of the message list.
acceptanceCriteria: TUI detects when the user scrolls to the top of the message list
Triggers sdk.client.session.messages({ sessionID, limit: 50, before: oldestMessageID }) to fetch older messages
A loading indicator appears while older messages are being fetched
Fetched older messages are prepended to the existing list without disrupting scroll position
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Depends on US-004 (server pagination). The TUI uses ink-based rendering. Scroll-to-top detection may require tracking viewport offset in the message list component.

userStories.title: Fork preserves full history for remote-attached sessions
userStories.description: As a user who attaches remotely and then forks the session, I want the forked session to contain the complete message history.
acceptanceCriteria: Session.fork() on the server always copies all messages regardless of client-side visibility
Verify fork works correctly when initiated by an observer-turned-owner
Fork does not depend on client-side message count — operates entirely on server-side data
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Session.fork() should already operate on server-side data. This story verifies the behavior and fixes if needed. May be a quick verification + minor fix.
